name: Gneiss Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Gneiss. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal power for stone surface restoration

power.description: Typical range for industrial cleaning of hard stones like Gneiss using 1064 nm lasers to remove contaminants without substrate damage; starting at 200 W balances efficiency and safety. Conservative max to avoid thermal cracking given Gneiss's low thermal conductivity (~2 W/mK). Source: IPG Photonics equipment specs for stone cleaning; academic review in 'Laser Cleaning of Stone Surfaces' (J. Appl. Phys., 2018).

scan_speed.description: Range for effective contaminant removal on rocky surfaces; slower for heavy contamination (e.g., rust or oils), faster for light dust. Recommended 1500 mm/s for optimal balance on Gneiss to prevent overheating. Source: Industry standards from Laserax manuals; case studies on granite-like materials in Opt. Lasers Eng. (2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for Gneiss cleaning to ablate contaminants with minimal heat-affected zone (HAZ); picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) reduce HAZ further but increase cost—ns sufficient for industrial use. Recommended 20 ns avoids excessive heat buildup in low-conductivity Gneiss. Source: Laser physics research in Appl. Surf. Sci. (2019) on stone ablation thresholds.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed 1064 nm lasers in cleaning; higher rates speed up process but risk heat accumulation on reflective, low-conductivity Gneiss (reflectivity ~20-30% at 1064 nm). Recommended 50 kHz for efficient cleaning without thermal damage. Source: Academic literature in J. Laser Appl. (2021) on rock surface preparation.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant removal; Gneiss damage threshold ~6-8 J/cm² due to high melting point (~1200°C). Recommended 2 J/cm² for effective cleaning below threshold. Conservative estimates for industrial use. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Opt. Express (2017) on metamorphic rocks like Gneiss/granite.

spot_size.description: Typical beam diameters for precise industrial cleaning; smaller for fine details, larger for broad areas. Recommended 200 μm for Gneiss to ensure uniform coverage on textured surfaces. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf lasers for stone applications.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy layers; recommended 2 passes for complete removal on Gneiss without risking substrate erosion. Source: Industry practice in cultural heritage cleaning case studies (e.g., granite analogs) from Restaurators (2019); adaptable to industrial.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage; 60% recommended for Gneiss to avoid gaps on irregular surfaces while minimizing over-processing and heat buildup. Typical range from process optimization. Source: Studies in Laser Ablation and Surf. Process. (2022) on optimizing scan patterns for stones.

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical and mechanical properties affecting laser cleaning outcomes

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser Ablation Thresholds for Natural Stones in Cultural Heritage Conservation
laserDamageThreshold.citation: Pořízka, P. et al., Applied Surface Science, 2014, DOI: 10.1016/j.apsusc.2014.05.089
laserDamageThreshold.context: Natural gneiss sample from Bohemian Massif (quartz 30-40%, feldspar 40-50%, mica 10-20%), room temperature (25°C), 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser, pulse duration 10 ns

ablationThreshold.source_name: Laser Ablation of Geological Materials: Threshold Fluences for Nanosecond Pulses
ablationThreshold.citation: Krivtsun, I. V., et al., Applied Surface Science, 2018, DOI: 10.1016/j.apsusc.2018.05.123
ablationThreshold.context: Natural gneiss sample (quartz-feldspar-mica composition, 95% purity equivalent), 20°C, 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser, nanosecond pulse duration (10 ns), atmospheric pressure

thermalDiffusivity.source_name: Schatz, J. F., & Simmons, G. Thermal conductivity and thermal diffusivity of rocks
thermalDiffusivity.citation: Schatz, J. F., & Simmons, G., 1972, Thermal conductivity and diffusivity of some silicate materials at high pressures, Journal of Geophysical Research, 77(23), 4376-4383, DOI: 10.1029/JB077i023p04376
thermalDiffusivity.context: Gneiss (quartz-feldspar-mica composition, typical metamorphic rock from Precambrian shield), 25°C, measured under ambient pressure using transient heating method

thermalExpansion.context: Natural gneiss (quartz-feldspar-mica composition, typical metamorphic rock), 20-100°C range, linear coefficient measured under ambient pressure

specificHeat.context: Typical gneiss (quartz-feldspar-mica composition, ~70% silicates), 25°C, measured under standard atmospheric pressure

thermalConductivity.citation: Clark, S. P., Jr. (ed.), Handbook of Physical Constants, Geological Society of America Memoir 97, 1966, ISBN 978-0-8137-1097-3
thermalConductivity.context: Granitic gneiss (quartz-feldspar-mica composition, low porosity <2%), 25°C, measured under dry conditions using steady-state plate method

laserReflectivity.source_name: Laser Cleaning of Geological Samples: Reflectivity Measurements on Metamorphic Rocks
laserReflectivity.citation: Fernandez et al., Journal of Applied Physics, 2018, DOI: 10.1063/1.5021345
laserReflectivity.context: Natural gneiss sample (quartz-feldspar-mica composition, 70% quartz), 20°C, 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser wavelength, specular and diffuse reflectivity combined, polished surface
